title: St. Ignatius
body: There are two guarantees of a wise rule of conduct: the thought before action, and self-command afterward.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: When we receive with an entire and perfect resignation the afflictions which God sends us they become for us favors and benefits; because conformity to the will of God is a gain far superior to all temporal advantages.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: All perfection consists in the love of God; and the perfection of divine love consists in the union of our will with that of God.

title: St. Thomas Aquinas
body: Leave to every one the care of what belongs to him, and disturb not thyself with what is said or done in the world.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Place before your eyes as models for imitation, not the weak and cowardly, but the fervent and courageous.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: Prayer is a pasturage, a field, wherein all the virtues find their nourishment, growth, and strength.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: A single act of resignation to the divine will in what it ordains contrary to our desires, is of more value than a hundred thousand successes conformable to our will and taste.

title: St. Ignatius
body: The shortest, yea, the only way to reach sanctity, is to conceive a horror for all that the world loves and values.

title: St. Teresa
body: As long as we are in this mortal life, nothing is more necessary for us than humility.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Learning without humility has always been pernicious to the Church; and as pride precipitated the rebellious angels from heaven, it frequently causes the loss of learned men.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: Why remain sad and idle? Why exhaust thyself in the anguish of melancholy? Have courage, do violence to thyself; meditate on the passion of Jesus Christ, and thou shalt overcome thy sorrow.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Here is the difference between the joys of the world and the cross of Jesus Christ: after having tasted the first, one is disgusted with them; and on the contrary, the more one partakes of the cross, the greater the thirst for it.

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: When the sky is free from clouds we can see more clearly the brightness of the sun. In like manner, when the soul is free from sin and the gloom of passion, it participates in the divine light.

title: St. Teresa
body: Our works are of no value if they be not united to the merits of Jesus Christ.

title: St. Teresa
body: If we are very determined to mortify ourselves and not to be too much occupied with our corporal health, we will soon, by the grace of God, become masters of our bodies.

title: Ven. Bartholomew of Martyrs
body: In every creature, however small it be, we may see a striking image of divine wisdom, power, and goodness.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: Time is but a period. It passes like the lightning flash. Suffering passes with time; suffering, then, is very short.

title: St. Teresa
body: In order to bear our afflictions with patience, it is very useful to read the lives and legends of the saints who endured great torments for Jesus Christ.

title: Ven. Louis of Granada
body: Open thine ears to the voices of nature, and thou shalt hear them in concert inviting thee to the love of God.

title: St. Teresa
body: On the feasts of the saints consider their virtues, and beseech God to deign to adorn you with them.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: When faith grows weak, all virtues are weakened. When faith is lost, all virtues are lost.

title: St. Ignatius
body: A precious crown is reserved in heaven for those who perform all their actions with all the diligence of which they are capable; for it is not sufficient to do our part well; it must be done more than well.

title: Unknown
body: Nothing created has ever been able to fill the heart of man. God alone can fill it infinitely.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Not sufficient to do our part well; it must be done more than well.

title: St. Thomas Aquinas
body: Nothing created has ever been able to fill the heart of man. God alone can fill it infinitely.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: We should only make use of life to grow in the love of God.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: In vain men try. They can never find in creatures sincere affection, perfect joy, or true peace.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: God is supreme strength, fortifying those who place their trust and confidence in Him.

title: St. Ignatius
body: God gives each one of us sufficient grace ever to know His holy will, and to do it fully.

title: St. Thomas Aquinas
body: Shun useless conversation. We lose by it both time and the spirit of devotion.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The upright intention is the soul of our actions. It gives them life and makes them good.

title: St. Teresa
body: The truth of faith alone, deeply graven in the soul, is sufficient to encourage us to very perfect works; for it strengthens man and increases his charity.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: It is folly not to think of death. It is greater folly to think of it, and not prepare for it.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The most perfect and meritorious intention is that by which, in all our actions, we have in view only the good pleasure of God and the accomplishment of His holy will.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Mary's sorrow was less when she saw her only Son crucified, than it is now at the sight of men offending Him by sin.

title: Ven. Louis of Granada
body: There is nothing more unreasonable than to estimate our worth by the opinion of others. Today they laud us to the skies, to-morrow they will cover us with ignominy.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Act as if every day were the last of your life, and each action the last you perform.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Perfection consists in renouncing ourselves, in carrying our cross, and in following Jesus Christ. Now, he who renounces himself most perfectly carries his cross the best and follows nearest to Jesus Christ is he who never does his own will, but always that of God.

title: St. Ignatius
body: That which would have easily been remedied at first, becomes incurable by time and habit.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Among the gifts of grace which the soul receives in holy communion there is one that must be numbered among the highest. It is, that holy communion does not permit the soul to remain long in sin, nor to obstinately persevere in it.

title: Ven. Louis of Granada
body: Be assured that one great means to find favor when we appear before God is to have pardoned the injuries we have received here below.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Woe to him who neglects to recommend himself to Mary, and thus closes the channel of grace!

title: Ven. Louis of Granada
body: It is folly to leave your goods where you can never return, and to send nothing to that place where you must remain for ever.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Discretion is necessary in spiritual life. It is its part to restrain the exercises in the way of perfection, so as to keep us between the two extremes.

title: St. Teresa
body: By denying our self-love and our inclinations in little things, we gradually acquire mortification and victory over ourselves.

title: Ven. Louis of Granada
body: Should we fall a thousand times in a day, a thousand times we must rise again, always animated with unbounded confidence in the infinite goodness of God.

title: St. Ignatius
body: God's way in dealing with those whom He intends to admit soonest after this life into the possession of His everlasting glory, is to purify them in this world by the greatest afflictions and trials.

title: St. Ignatius
body: In this world by the greatest afflictions and trials.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: After the flower comes the fruit: we receive, as the reward of our fatigues, an increase of grace in this world, and in the next the eternal vision of God.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: God refuses no one the gift of prayer. By it we obtain the help that we need to overcome disorderly desires and temptations of all kinds.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: To establish ourselves in a virtue it is necessary to form good and practical resolutions to perform certain and determined acts of that virtue, and we must, moreover, be faithful in executing them.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Love ought to consist of deeds more than of words.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: There are many things which seem to us misfortunes and which we call such; but if we understood the designs of God we would call them graces.

title: Bl. Albert the Great
body: Let us abandon everything to the merciful providence of God.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: Jesus Christ, our great Model, suffered much for us; let us bear our afflictions cheerfully, seeing that through them we have the happiness of resembling Him.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: Remember that virtue is a very high and rugged mountain, difficult to ascend, and requiring much fatigue and exertion before we arrive at the summit to rest.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Labor to conquer yourself. This victory will assure you a brighter crown in heaven than they gain whose disposition is more amiable.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We should not examine articles of faith with a curious and subtle spirit. It is sufficient for us to know that the Church proposes them. We can never be deceived in believing them.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We should guard against jealousy, and even the slightest sentiment thereof. This vice is absolutely opposed to a pure and sincere zeal for the glory of God, and is a certain proof of secret and subtle pride.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: Charity requires us always to have compassion on human infirmity.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: When one does not love prayer, it is morally impossible for him to resist his passions.

title: Ven. Julienne Morel
body: Docility and easy acquiescence with good advice are the signs of a humble heart.

title: Bl. Bartholomew of Martyrs
body: There is nothing richer, nothing surer, nothing more agreeable than a good conscience.

title: St. Teresa
body: It seems as if God granted to other saints to free us from some particular needfulness; but I know by experience that the glorious St. Joseph assists us generally in all our necessities.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: A most powerful and efficacious remedy for all evils, a means of correcting all imperfections, of triumphing over temptation, and preserving our hearts in an undisturbed peace, is conformity with the will of God.

title: St. Teresa
body: It often happens that when we take less care of our body, we have better health than when we bestow upon it too much care.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Do nothing, say nothing before considering if that which you are about to say or do is pleasing to God, profitable to yourself, and edifying to your neighbor.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: Sometimes God leaves us for a long time unable to effect any good, that we may learn to humble ourselves, and never to glory in our efforts.

title: Bl. Bartholomew of Martyrs
body: We easily lose peace of mind, because we make it depend, not on the testimony of a good conscience, but on the judgment of men.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: You may fast regularly, give alms, and pray without ceasing, but as long as you hate your brother, you will not be numbered among the children of God.

title: LOUIS DE BLOIS
body: Easing, but as long as you hate your brother, you will not be numbered among the children of God.

title: ST. TERESA
body: He who at the hour of death finds himself protected by St. Joseph will certainly experience great consolation.

title: ST. IGNATIUS
body: Take care that the worldling does not pursue with greater zeal and anxiety the perishable goods of this world than you do the eternal.

title: ST. VINCENT DE PAUL
body: We should consider our departed brethren as living members of Jesus Christ, animated by His grace, and certain of participating one day of His glory. We should therefore love, serve, and assist them as far as is in our power.

title: BL. HENRY SUSO
body: Control thy senses, guard thy mouth, bridle thy tongue, subjugate thy heart, bear all provocation with charity, and thou shalt perfectly fulfill the will of God.

title: ST. VINCENT DE PAUL
body: Our perfection consists in uniting our will so intimately with God's will that we will only desire what He wills. He who conforms most perfectly to the will of God will be the most perfect Christian.

title: BL. JORDAN OF SAXONY
body: Humility, modesty, sobriety, purity, piety, and prudence, with meekness, ornament the soul and make us live on earth a truly angelic life.

title: ST. THOMAS AQUINAS
body: In recalling to mind the life and actions of the saints, walk in their footsteps as much as possible, and humble thyself if thou canst not attain to their perfection.

title: ST. ALPHONSUS
body: When the devil again tempts you to sin, telling you that God is merciful, remember that the Lord showeth mercy to them that fear Him, but not to them who despise Him.

title: ST. VINCENT DE PAUL
body: In prayer we should particularly combat our predominant passion or evil inclination. We should devote continual attention to it, because when it is once conquered we will easily obtain the victory over all our other faults.

title: ST. IGNATIUS
body: I will carefully consider how, on the day of judgment, I would wish to have discharged my office or my duty; and the way I would wish to have done it then I shall do now.

title: ST. BENEDICT
body: It is well to deny ourselves that which is permitted, in order to avoid more easily that which is not.

title: ST. TERESA
body: I have noticed that all persons who have true devotion to St. Joseph and tender him special honor are very much advanced in virtue, for he takes great care of souls who recommend themselves to him; and I have never asked of him anything which he did not obtain for me.

title: ST. IGNATIUS
body: He who forgets himself in the service of God may be assured that God will not forget Him.

title: ST. BENEDICT
body: Let all our actions be directed to the end that God may be glorified in all things.

title: ST. ALPHONSUS
body: He who suffers in patience suffers less and saves his soul. He who suffers impatiently suffers more and loses his soul.

title: ST. TERESA
body: When we remember or hear that the enemies of the Church burn and destroy God's temples, we should grieve therefor; but we should also rejoice much when we see new ones built, and we should co-operate in their erection as much as we possibly can.

title: ST. TERESA
body: We should carefully beware of giving ourselves so completely to any employment as to forget to have recourse to God from time to time.

title: ST. IGNATIUS
body: Our Lady, deign to intercede for us sinners with thy divine Son, our Lord, and obtain of Him a blessing for us in our trials and tribulations!

title: ST. TERESA
body: Whoever would follow Jesus Christ must walk in His footsteps, if he would not go astray.

title: ST. ALPHONSUS
body: Let us thank God for having called us to His holy faith. It is a great gift, and the number of those who thank God for it is small.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Let us thank God for having called us to His holy faith. It is a great gift, and the number of those who thank God for it is small.

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: The trials of life cease to oppress us if we accept them for the love of God.

title: St. Benedict
body: If you wish to take up your abode in the tabernacle of the heavenly kingdom, you must reach there through your good works, without which you cannot hope to enter.

title: St. Teresa
body: It is a great folly to be willing to violate the friendship of God, rather than the law of human friendship.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: When the afflictions of this life overcome us, let us encourage ourselves to bear them patiently by the hope of heaven.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: To put into practice the teachings of our holy faith, it is not enough to convince ourselves that they are true; we must love them. Love united to faith makes us practice our religion.

title: St. Teresa
body: Unite all your works to the merits of Jesus Christ, and then offer them up to the eternal Father if you desire to make them pleasing to Him.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: God pardons sin; but He will not pardon the will to sin.

title: St. Bernard
body: It is a fault, not a virtue, to wish your humility recognized and applauded.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: Before engaging in your private devotions, perform those which obedience and your duty toward your neighbor impose upon you in such a manner as to make an abnegation of self.

title: Bl. John Tauler
body: The world is full of inconstancy; its friendship ceases the moment there is no advantage to be expected from us.

title: St. Benedict
body: There is nothing better to display the truth in an excellent light than a clear and simple statement of facts.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Be careful and do not lightly condemn the actions of others. We must consider the intention of our neighbor, which is often good and pure, although the act itself seems blameworthy.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: He who does not overcome his predominant passion is in great danger of being lost. He who does overcome it will easily conquer all the rest.

title: St. Ignatius
body: To conquer himself is the greatest victory that man can gain.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: A soul which does not practice the exercise of prayer is very like a paralyzed body which, though possessing feet and hands, makes no use of them.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: When you do a good action, have the intention of first pleasing God, and then of giving good example to your neighbor.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: The grace of perseverance is the most important of all; it crowns all other graces.

title: St. Teresa
body: Prayer is the only channel through which God's great graces and favors may flow into the soul; and if this be once closed, I know no other way He can communicate them.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: To acquire courage it is very useful to read the lives of the saints, especially of those who, after living in sin, attained great sanctity.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The truly humble reject all praise for themselves, and refer it all to God.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Prayer should be effective and practical, since it has for its end the acquisition of solid virtue and the mortification of the passions.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: We do not keep an account of the graces which God has given us, but God our Lord keeps an account of them. He has fixed the measure thereof.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: The more guilty we are, the greater must be our confidence in Mary. Therefore, courage, timid soul; let Mary know all thy misery, and hasten with joy to the throne of mercy.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: Evil is often more hurtful to the doer than to the one against whom it is done.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: Evil is often more hurtful to the doer than to the one against whom it is done.

title: St. Anselm
body: During life despise that which will avail you nothing at the hour of death.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: He who fails to reflect before acting, walks with his eyes shut and advances with danger. He also falls very often, because the eye of reflection does not enable him to see whither his footsteps lead.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: Sanctity and perfection consist not in fine words, but in good actions.

title: St. Bernard
body: As patience leads to peace, and study to science, so are humiliations the path that leads to humility.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: Do not disturb yourself with vain curiosity concerning the affairs of others, nor how they conduct themselves, unless your position makes it your duty to do so.

title: St. Bernard
body: The deceitful charms of prosperity destroy more souls than all the scourges of adversity.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: The first degree of humility is the fear of God, which we should constantly have before our eyes.

title: St. Mechtildis
body: He who cheerfully endures contempt and is happy under crosses and affliction, partakes of the humility and sufferings of Our Lord.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: He who is resigned to the divine will shall always surmount the difficulties he meets with in the service of God. The Lord will accomplish His designs concerning him.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: Consent to suffer a slight temporary pain, that so thou mayst avoid the eternal pains which sin deserves.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Mary was the most perfect among the saints only because she was always perfectly united to the will of God.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: After the love which we owe Jesus Christ, we must give the chief place in our heart to the love of His Mother Mary.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: When we feel our cross weighing upon us, let us have recourse to Mary, whom the Church calls the 'Consoler of the Afflicted.'

title: St. Teresa
body: The devotions we practise in honor of the glorious Virgin Mary, however trifling they be, are very pleasing to her divine Son, and He rewards them with eternal glory.

title: St. Teresa
body: There is nothing which is more profitable and more consoling to the mind than to frequently remember the Blessed Virgin.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Blessed are the actions enclosed between two Hail Marys.

title: St. Teresa
body: Let us consider what the glorious Virgin endured, and what the holy apostles suffered, and we shall find that they who were nearest to Jesus Christ were the most afflicted.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The servants of Mary who are in purgatory receive visits and consolations from her.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: If you persevere until death in true devotion to Mary, your salvation is certain.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: He who remembers having invoked the name of Mary in an impure temptation, may be sure that he did not yield to it.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Mary being destined to negotiate peace between God and man, it was not proper that she should be an accomplice in the disobedience of Adam.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Mary having co-operated in our redemption with so much glory to God and so much love for us, Our Lord ordained that no one shall obtain salvation except through her intercession.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: He who wishes to find Jesus will do so only by having recourse to Mary.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Mary having always lived wholly detached from earthly things and united with God, death, which united her more closely to Him, was extremely sweet and agreeable to her.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Mary being in heaven nearer to God and more united to Him, knows our miseries better, compassionates them more, and can more efficaciously assist us.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The Virgin Mother, all pure and all white, will make her servants pure and white.

title: St. Teresa
body: To assure our salvation it does not suffice to call ourselves children of Mary; therefore let us always have the fear of God.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Let us offer ourselves without delay and without reserve to Mary, and beg her to offer us herself to God.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Such is the compassion, such the love which Mary bears us, that she is never tired of praying for us.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: O Queen of heaven and earth! The universe would perish before thou couldst refuse aid to one who invokes thee from the depth of his heart.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: O most blessed Virgin, who declarest in thy Canticle that it is owing to thy humility that God hath done great things in thee, obtain for me the grace to imitate thee, that is, to be obedient; because to obey is to practise humility.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: May the two names so sweet and so powerful, of Jesus and Mary, be always in our hearts and on our lips!

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Whatsoever we do, we can never be true children of Mary, unless we are humble.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Let us highly esteem devotion to the Blessed Virgin, and let us lose no opportunity of inspiring others with it.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: As a mother feels no disgust in dressing the sores of her child, so Mary, the heavenly infirmarian, never refuses to care for sinners who have recourse to her.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Each of our days is marked with the protection of Mary, who is exceedingly anxious to be our Mother, when we desire to be her children.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: When the devil wishes to make himself master of a soul, he seeks to make it give up devotion to Mary.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: Let us have recourse to Mary; for of all creatures she is the highest, the purest, the most beautiful, and the most loving.

title: St. Bernard
body: Let the name of Mary be ever on your lips, let it be indelibly engraven on your heart. If you are under her protection, you have nothing to fear; if she is propitious, you will arrive at the port of salvation.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Know that of all devotions the most pleasing to Mary is to have frequent recourse to her, asking for favors.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Let the servants of Mary perform every day, and especially on Saturday, some work of charity for her sake.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Can we, amongst all hearts, find one more amiable than that of Jesus? It is on His Heart that God looks with special complacency.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: One must wage war against his predominant passion, and not retreat, until, with God's help, he has been victorious.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: An act of perfect conformity to the will of God unites us more to Him than a hundred other acts of virtue.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: The love of God inspires the love of our neighbor, and the love of our neighbor serves to keep alive the love of God.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: Live always in the certainty that whatever happens to you is the result of divine Providence; because nothing hard or laborious falls to your lot without the Lord permitting it.

title: St. Benedict
body: Whatsoever good work you undertake, pray earnestly to God that He will enable you to bring it to a successful termination.

title: St. Bernard
body: What is a fruitless repentance, defiled almost immediately by new faults?

title: St. Bernard
body: What is a fruitless repentance, defiled almost immediately by new faults?

title: St. Benedict
body: You propose to give up everything to God; be sure, then, to include yourself among the things to be given up.

title: St. Anselm
body: If you can find a place where God is not, go there and sin with impunity.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: He can not err who is constantly with the visible Head which Jesus Christ has left to His Church, as its foundation, rule, teacher, and defender of the Faith.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: The more numerous the gifts we have received from God, the greater the account we must render to Him.

title: St. Bernard
body: True penance consists in regretting without ceasing the faults of the past, and in firmly resolving to never again commit that which is so deplorable.

title: St. Bernard
body: We are not raised the first day to the summit of perfection. It is by climbing, not by flying, that we arrive there.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: What we do for ourselves during life is more certain than all the good we expect others to do for us after death.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Idleness begets a discontented life. It develops self-love, which is the cause of all our misery, and renders us unworthy to receive the favors of divine love.

title: St. Bernard
body: Have death always before your eyes as a salutary means of returning to God.

title: St. Ignatius
body: If the devil tempts me by the thought of divine justice, I think of God's mercy; if he tries to fill me with presumption by the thought of His mercy, I think of His justice.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: In time of temptation continue the good thou hast begun before temptation.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: In the eyes of the sovereign Judge the merit of our actions depends on the motives which prompted them.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: The benefits to be derived from spiritual reading do not merely consist in impressing on the memory the precepts set forth, but in opening the heart to them, that they may bear fruit.

title: Ven. Louis of Granada
body: As clouds obscure the sun, so bad thoughts darken and destroy the brightness of the soul.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: To judge rightly of the goodness and perfection of anyone's prayer, it is sufficient to know the disposition he takes to it, and the fruits he reaps from it.

title: Blessed Henry Suso
body: To commence many things and not to finish them is no small fault; we must persevere in whatever we undertake with upright intention and according to God's will.

title: Ven. John Tauler
body: The perfect champion is he who establishes complete control over his mind by overcoming temptations and the inclination of his nature to sin.

title: St. Ignatius
body: If the love of God is in your heart, you will understand that to suffer for God is a joy to which all earthly pleasures are not to be compared.

title: Ven. Bartholomew of Martyrs
body: The world around us is, as it were, a book written by the finger of God; every creature is a word on the page. We should apply ourselves well to understand the signification of the volume.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: A man of prayer is capable of everything. He can say with St. Paul, "I can do all things in Him who strengthened me."

title: Ven. John Tauler
body: Whilst here below our actions can never be entirely free from negligence, frailty, or defect; but we must not throw away the wheat because of the chaff.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: Strive always to preserve freedom of spirit, so that you need do nothing with the view of pleasing the world, and that no fear of displeasing it will have power to shake your good resolutions.

title: Unknown
body: Wo to us poor sinners if we had not the Divine Sacrifice to ap

title: St. Ignatius
body: How few there are who avail themselves of the precious blood of Jesus to purchase their salvation!

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: O Queen of heaven and earth! Thou art the gate of mercy ever open, never closed. The universe must perish before he who invokes thee from his heart is refused assistance.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Our Faith will never be true unless it is united to that of St. Peter and the Pontiff, his successors.

title: Ven. John Tauler
body: Short pleasures and long sufferings are all the world can give.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: Learn to be silent sometimes for the edification of others, that you may learn how to speak sometimes.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Gratitude for graces received is a most efficacious means of obtaining new ones.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: To a useless question we should answer only by silence.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We should not judge things by their exterior or appearance, but consider what they are in the sight of God, and whether they be according to His good pleasure.

title: St. Thomas Aquinas
body: Preserve purity of conscience with care, and never do anything to sully it or render it less agreeable to God.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: Give not thyself too much to any one. He who gives himself too freely is generally the least acceptable.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: Affliction strengthens the vigor of our soul, whereas happiness weakens it.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: To acquire purity of the soul, it is necessary to guard against passing judgment on our neighbor, or useless remarks on his conduct.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: Turn away the eyes of thy body and those of thy mind from seeing others, that thou mayest be able to contemplate thyself.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The brightest ornaments in the crown of the blessed in heaven are the sufferings which they have borne patiently on earth.

title: St. Bernard
body: We are not innocent before God if we punish that which we should pardon, or pardon that which we should punish.

title: St. Bernard
body: Is there any one in the world who has invoked thee, O Mary, without having felt the benefit of thy protection, which is promised to those who invoke thy mercy?

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: It is the key of obedience that opens the door of paradise. Jesus Christ has confided that key to His vicar, the Pope, Christ on earth, whom all are obliged to obey even unto death.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: It is true that God promises forgiveness if we repent, but what assurance have we of obtaining it to-morrow?

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We should offer ourselves and all we have to God, that He may dispose of us according to His holy will, so that we may be ever ready to leave all and embrace the afflictions that come upon us.

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: No one has a right to mercy who can not himself show mercy.

title: Ven. John Tauler
body: We should reflect on all our actions, exterior and interior, and before we commence, examine well if we are able to finish them.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The reason why the lukewarm run so great a risk of being lost is because tepidity conceals from the soul the immense evil which it causes.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We should learn of Jesus Christ to be meek and humble of heart, and ask Him unceasingly for these two virtues. We ought, particularly, to avoid the two contrary vices which would cause us to destroy with one hand what we seek to raise with the other.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Contrary vices which would cause us to destroy with one hand what we seek to raise with the other.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The sufferings endured for God are the greatest proof of our love for Him.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: It is in vain that we cut off the branches of evil, if we leave intact the root, which continually produces new ones.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: How little is required to be a saint! It suffices to do in all things the will of God.

title: Ven. Bartholomew of Martyrs
body: Wouldst thou know what thou art? Thou art that to which thy heart turns the most frequently.

title: St. Bernard
body: When you covet that which delights you, think not only of the sweet moments of enjoyment, but of the long season of regret which must follow.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: They who voluntarily commit sin show a contempt for life eternal, since they willingly risk the loss of their soul.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: It suffices not to perform good works; we must do them well, in imitation of Our Lord Jesus Christ, of whom it is written, 'He doeth all things well.'

title: St. Ignatius
body: Put not off till to-morrow what you can do today.

title: St. Antoninus
body: Christ Himself guides the bark of Peter. For this reason, it cannot perish, although He sometimes seems to sleep.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Prayer teaches us the need of laying before God all our necessities, of corresponding with His grace, of banishing vice from our heart and of establishing virtue in it.

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: Take this to heart: Owe no man anything. So shalt thou secure a peaceful sleep, an easy conscience, a life without inquietude, and a death without alarm.

title: St. Bernard
body: If you would know whether you have made a good confession, ask yourself if you have resolved to abandon your sins.

title: St. Anselm
body: He who does that which is displeasing to himself has discovered the secret of pleasing God.

title: Ven. Louis de Blois
body: An ordinary action, performed through obedience and love of God, is more meritorious than extraordinary works done on your own authority.

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: Vigilance is rendered necessary and indispensable, not only by the dangers that surround us, but by the delicacy, the extreme difficulty of the work we all have to engage in the work of our salvation.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Among the different means that we have of pleasing God in all that we do, one of the most efficacious is to perform each of our actions as though it were to be the last of our life.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: I have to seek only the glory of God, my own sanctification, and the salvation of my neighbor. I should therefore devote myself to these things, if necessary, at the peril of my life.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Idleness is hell's fishhook for catching souls.

title: St. Antoninus
body: Whoever imagines himself without defect has an excess of pride. God alone is perfect.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: As we take the bitterest medicine to recover or preserve the health of the body, we should cheerfully endure sufferings, however repugnant to nature, and consider them efficacious remedies which God employs to purify the soul and conduct it to the perfection to which He called it.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: To give up prayer because we are often distracted at it is to allow the devil to gain his cause.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Curb the desire of display, and do nothing from human respect.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: O Mary, vessel of purest gold, ornamented with pearls and sapphires, filled with grace and virtue, thou art the dearest of all creatures to the eyes of eternal Wisdom.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: We must be careful not to omit our prayers, confession, communion, and other exercises of piety, even when we find no consolation in them.

title: Ven. John Tauler
body: Let us leave to God and to truth the care of our justification, without trying to excuse ourselves, and peace will truly spring up within us.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Read good and useful books, and abstain from reading those that only gratify curiosity.

title: St. Bernard
body: So great is the goodness of God in your regard, that when you ask through ignorance for that which is not beneficial, He does not grant your prayer in this matter, but gives you something better instead.

title: St. Teresa
body: Men can use no better arms to drive away the devil, than prayer and the sign of the cross.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: He who knows well how to practise the exercise of the presence of God, and who is faithful in following the attraction of this divine virtue, will soon attain a very high degree of perfection.

title: St. Ignatius
body: One of the most admirable effects of holy communion is to preserve the soul from sin, and to help those who fall through weakness to rise again. It is much more profitable, then, to approach this divine Sacrament with love, respect, and confidence, than to remain away through an excess of fear and scrupulosity.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Let us remember that every act of mortification is a work for heaven. This thought will make all suffering and weariness sweet.

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: Correction should be given calmly and with discernment, at seasonable times, according to the dictates of reason, and not at the impulse of anger.

title: Ven. Bartholomew of Martyrs
body: There is nothing more certain, nothing more agreeable, nothing richer than a good conscience.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: God, to procure His glory, sometimes permits that we should be dishonored and persecuted without reason. He wishes thereby to render us conformable to His Son, who was calumniated and treated as a seducer, as an ambitious man, and as one possessed.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: All that God gives us and all that He permits in this world have no other end than to sanctify us in Him.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: If you cannot mortify your body by actual penance, abstain at least from some lawful pleasure.

title: Ven. Julienne Morel
body: One whose heart is embittered can do nothing but contend and contradict, finding something to oppose in every remark.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Without prayer we have neither light nor strength to advance in the way which leads to God.

title: Bl. Albert the Great
body: I have never gone out to mingle with the world without losing something of myself.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: He who perseveres with constancy and fervor will, without fail, raise himself to a high degree of perfection.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: An upright intention is the soul of our actions. It gives them life, and makes them good.

title: St. Ignatius
body: You wish to reform the world: reform yourself, otherwise your efforts will be in vain.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: Let all thy care be to possess thy soul in peace and tranquillity. Let no accident be to thee a cause of ill-humor.

title: Ven. Louis of Granada
body: Humility is a fortified town; it repels all attacks. The sight of it obliges the enemy to turn and flee.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: The world is deceitful and inconstant. When fortune forsakes us, friendship takes flight.

title: St. Mechtildis
body: Perform all your actions in union with the pure intention and perfect love with which Our Lord did all things for the glory of God and the salvation of the world.

title: Ven. John Tauler
body: An air of meekness and a modest speech are pleasing alike to God and men.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The saints owed to their confidence in God that unalterable tranquillity of soul, which procured their perpetual joy and peace, even in the midst of adversities.

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: Look not to the qualities thou mayest possess, which are wanting to others; but look to those which others possess and which are wanting to thee, that thou mayest acquire them.

title: St. Ignatius
body: Your heart is not so narrow that the world can satisfy it entirely; nothing but God can fill it.

title: St. Thomas Aquinas
body: If you wish to raise a lofty edifice of perfection, take humility for a foundation.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: It ordinarily happens that God permits those who judge others to fall into the same or even greater faults.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: Raise thy heart and thy love toward the sweet and most holy cross, which soothes every pain!

title: St. Antoninus
body: Often read spiritual books; then, like a sheep, ruminate the food thou hast taken, by meditation and a desire to practise the holy doctrine found therein.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: Love others much, but visit them seldom.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: God sends us trials and afflictions to exercise us in patience and teach us sympathy with the sorrows of others.

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: Armed with prayer, the saints sustained a glorious warfare and vanquished all their enemies. By prayer, also, they appeased the wrath of God, and obtained from Him all they desired.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: All souls in hell are there because they did not pray. All the saints sanctified themselves by prayer.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: The thought of the presence of God renders us familiar with the practice of doing in all things His holy will.

title: St. Vincent Ferrer
body: If we consider the number and excellence of the virtues practised by the saints, we must feel the inefficiency and imperfection of our actions.

title: St. Bernard
body: Prayer without fervor has not sufficient strength to rise to heaven.

title: Ven. Louis of Granada
body: The path of virtue is painful to nature when left to itself; but nature, assisted by grace, finds it easy and agreeable.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Always give the preference to actions which appear to you the most agreeable to God, and most contrary to self-love.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: As the branch separated from the roots soon loses all life and verdure, so it is with good works which are not united with charity.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: We should constantly thank the Lord for having granted us the gift of the true faith, by associating us with the children of the holy Catholic Church.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We should not spare expense, fatigue, nor even our life, when there is a question of accomplishing the holy will of God.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Some are unable to fast or give alms; there are none who cannot pray.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We meet with contradictions everywhere. If only two persons are together they mutually afford each other opportunities of exercising patience, and even when one is alone there will still be a necessity for this virtue, so true it is that our miserable life is full of crosses.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: We should bear our sufferings in expiation for our sins, to merit heaven, and to please God.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: ALWAYS give good example: teach virtue by word and deed. Example is more powerful than discourse.

title: Ven. Louis De Granada
body: If thou wouldst glory, let it be in the Lord, by referring everything to Him, and giving to Him all the honor and glory.

title: St. Vincent De Paul
body: There is nothing more holy, more eminently perfect, than resignation to the will of God, which confirms us in an entire detachment from ourselves, and a perfect indifference for every condition in which we may be placed.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: Prayer consists not in many words, but in the fervor of desire, which raises the soul to God by the knowledge of its own nothingness and the divine goodness.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Let us make up for lost time. Let us give to God the time that remains to us.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: When thou feelest thyself excited, shut thy mouth and chain thy tongue.

title: Ven. John Tauler
body: If it was necessary that Christ should suffer and so enter by the cross into the kingdom of His Father, no friend of God should shrink from suffering.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: We should grieve to see no account made of time, which is so precious; to see it employed so badly, so uselessly, for it can never be recalled.

title: St. Vincent De Paul
body: Every time that some unexpected event befalls us, be it affliction, or be it spiritual or corporal consolation, we should endeavor to receive it with equanimity of spirit, since all comes from the hand of God.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: There are some who sin through frailty, or through the force of some violent passion. They desire to break these chains of death; if their prayer is constant they will be heard.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: "Thy will be done!" This is what the saints had continually on their lips and in their hearts.

title: Ven. John Tauler
body: He who would be a disciple of Jesus Christ must live in sufferings; for "The servant is not greater than the Master."

title: St. Vincent De Paul
body: He who submits himself to God in all things is certain that whatever men say or do against him will always turn to his advantage.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: If he be blind who refuses to believe in the truths of the Catholic faith, how much blinder is he who believes, and yet lives as if he did not believe!

title: St. Teresa
body: There is no affliction, trial, or labor difficult to endure, when we consider the torments and sufferings which Our Lord Jesus Christ endured for us.

title: St. Catherine Of Siena
body: Outside of God nothing is durable. We exchange life for death, health for sickness, honor for shame, riches for poverty. All things change and pass away.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: If you would keep yourself pure, shun dangerous occasions. Do not trust your own strength. In this matter we cannot take too much precaution.

title: St. Vincent De Paul
body: After knowing the will of God in regard to a work which we undertake, we should continue courageously, however difficult it may be. We should follow it to the end with as much constancy as the obstacles we encounter are great.

title: Ven. Louis De Blois
body: In your prayers, if you would quickly and surely draw upon you the grace of God, pray in a special manner for our Holy Church and all those connected with it.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Prayer is our principal weapon. By it we obtain of God the victory over our evil inclinations, and over all temptations of hell.

title: St. Vincent De Paul
body: We should never abandon, on account of the difficulties we encounter, an enterprise undertaken with due reflection.

title: St. Vincent De Paul
body: Being all members of the same body, with the same head, who is Christ, it is pr

title: St. Alphonsus
body: When we have to reply to someone who speaks harshly to us, we must always do it with gentleness. If we are angry, it is better to keep silence.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The two principal dispositions which we should bring to holy communion are detachment from creatures, and the desire to receive Our Lord with a view to loving Him more in the future.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: In doing penance it is necessary to deprive oneself of as many lawful pleasures as we had the misfortune to indulge in unlawful ones.

title: St. Thomas Aquinas
body: In raising human nature to heaven by His ascension, Christ has given us the hope of arriving thither ourselves.

title: St. Benedict
body: It is useless to subdue the flesh by abstinence, unless one gives up his irregular life, and abandons vices which defile his soul.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: No prayers are so acceptable to God as those which we offer Him after communion.

title: St. Gregory the Great
body: It avails nothing to subdue the body, if the mind allows itself to be controlled by anger.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: What is it that renders death terrible? Sin. We must therefore fear sin, not death.

title: St. Peter Damian
body: The Blessed Virgin is of all the works of the Creator the most excellent, and to find anything in nature more grand one must go to the Author of nature Himself.

title: St. Teresa
body: If we would advance in virtue, we must not neglect little things, for they pave the way to greater.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: When one has fallen into some fault, what better remedy can there be than to have immediate recourse to the Most Blessed Sacrament?

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Afflictions are the most certain proofs that God can give us of His love for us.

title: St. Catherine of Siena
body: Is it not a great cruelty for us Christians, members of the body of the Holy Church, to attack one another?

title: Ven. Louis de Granada
body: The Church is the pillar and ground of truth, and her infallibility admits of no doubt.

title: St. Teresa
body: He who truly loves his neighbor and cannot efficaciously assist him, should strive at least to relieve and help him by his prayers.

title: St. Teresa
body: We should blush for shame to show so much resentment at what is done or said against us, knowing that so many injuries and affronts have been offered to our Redeemer and the saints.

title: St. Teresa
body: The reason why so many souls who apply themselves to prayer are not inflamed with God's love is that they neglect to carefully prepare themselves for it.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: It is absolutely necessary, both for our advancement and the salvation of others, to follow always and in all things the beautiful light of faith.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: If we consider all that is imperfect and worldly in us, we shall find ample reason for abasing ourselves before God and man, before ourselves and our inferiors.

title: St. Teresa
body: No one should think or say anything of another which he would not wish thought or said of himself.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We should study the interests of others as our own, and be careful to act on all occasions with uprightness and loyalty.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: It is God Himself who receives what we give in charity, and is it not an incomparable happiness to give Him what belongs to Him, and what we have received from His goodness alone?

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Let your constant practice be to offer yourself to God, that He may do with you as He wills.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Let your constant practice be to offer yourself to God, that He may do with you what He pleases.

title: Louis de Granada
body: It is not enough to forbid our own tongue to murmur; we must also refuse to listen to murmurers.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: We can obtain no reward without merit, and no merit without patience.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: No harp sends forth such sweet harmonies as are produced in the afflicted heart by the holy name of Mary. Let us kneel to reverence this holy, this sublime name of Mary!

title: St. Teresa
body: The life of a true Christian should be such that he fears neither death nor any event of his life, but endures and submits to all things with a good heart.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: We should abandon ourselves entirely into the hands of God, and believe that His providence disposes everything that He wishes or permits to happen to us for our greater good.

title: St. Teresa
body: Regulate and direct all your actions to God, offering them to Him and beseeching Him to grant that they be for His honor and glory.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Conformity to the will of God is an easy and certain means of acquiring a great treasure of graces in this life.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: Do not consider what others do, or how they do it; for there are but few who really work for their own sanctification.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: To-day God invites you to do good; do it therefore to-day. To-morrow you may not have time, or God may no longer call you to do it.

title: St. Teresa
body: To advance in the way of perfection it does not suffice to say a number of weak prayers; our principal care should be to acquire solid virtues.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Humility is the virtue of Our Lord Jesus Christ, of His blessed Mother, and of the greatest saints. It embraces all virtues and, where it is sincere, introduces them into the soul.

title: St. Teresa
body: It will be a great consolation for us at the hour of death to know that we are to be judged by Him whom we have loved above all things during life.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: Humble submission and obedience to the decrees of the Sovereign Pontiffs are good means for distinguishing the loyal from the rebellious children of the Church.

title: Bl. Henry Suso
body: The devil attacks us at the time of prayer more frequently than at other times. His object is to make us weary of prayer.

title: St. Ignatius
body: It is an act as rare as it is precious, to transact business with many people, without ever forgetting God or oneself.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: God is our light. The farther the soul strays away from God, the deeper it goes into darkness.

title: St. Vincent de Paul
body: True Christian prudence makes us submit our intellect to the maxims of the Gospel without fear of being deceived. It teaches us to judge things as Jesus Christ judged them, and to speak and act as He did.

title: St. Teresa
body: Remember that men change easily, and that you can not place your trust in them; therefore attach yourself to God alone.

title: St. Teresa
body: If we secretly feel a desire to appear greater or better than others, we must repress it at once.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: The King of heaven deigned to be born in a stable, because He came to destroy pride, the cause of man's ruin.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: To save our souls we must live according to the maxims of the Gospel, and not according to those of the world.

title: St. Teresa
body: Be gentle and kind with every one, and severe with yourself.

title: St. Alphonsus
body: If you wish to be pleasing to God and happy here below, be in all things united to His will.
